top_numeric_drivers_for_high_priority.interpretation: Higher fill rate means the supplier is more dependable at replenishment. In this model view it increases the signal for High stocking priority.
Long shelf life reduces spoilage pressure and supports stocking confidence. In this model view it increases the signal for High stocking priority.
Price shifts both perceived value and demand sensitivity. In this model view it increases the signal for High stocking priority.
Website traffic helps represent broader customer attention. In this model view it increases the signal for High stocking priority.
Longer lead times make replenishment harder and usually lower confidence. In this model view it increases the signal for High stocking priority.
Weather can shift store traffic and short-term consumption patterns. In this model view it increases the signal for High stocking priority.

top_numeric_drivers_for_low_priority.interpretation: Supplier delays weaken confidence in keeping the item available. In this model view it increases the signal for Low stocking priority.
Relative price position affects competitiveness in-store. In this model view it increases the signal for Low stocking priority.
Discounts can help demand but should still be weighed against margin. In this model view it increases the signal for Low stocking priority.
Shelf capacity affects how much space the item consumes. In this model view it increases the signal for Low stocking priority.
Higher marketing support can strengthen expected pull from shoppers. In this model view it increases the signal for Low stocking priority.
Repeat purchase rate is a signal of product stickiness. In this model view it increases the signal for Low stocking priority.

top_numeric_drivers_for_sales.interpretation: Higher footfall indicates stronger shopper traffic around the product. In this model view it increases the signal for higher daily sales.
Relative price position affects competitiveness in-store. In this model view it increases the signal for higher daily sales.
Loyalty usage reflects engaged repeat shoppers. In this model view it increases the signal for higher daily sales.
Basket size gives context on cross-sell and shopping missions. In this model view it increases the signal for higher daily sales.
Website traffic helps represent broader customer attention. In this model view it increases the signal for higher daily sales.
App activity acts as a digital demand signal. In this model view it increases the signal for higher daily sales.

interpretability_notes: Coefficient-based interpretation is especially useful here because both deployed models are linear.
Text tokens show semantic cues, while numeric coefficients connect directly to business levers such as fill rate, shelf life, and marketing spend.
Because numeric features are standardized, coefficient magnitudes are comparable within a model family.
The insights script refits analysis-only copies in the current environment so artifact generation stays stable even if saved sklearn versions differ.
